expectations.text: Output includes a coverage matrix mapping design elements to test cases
expectations.evidence: The file contains no coverage matrix, traceability table, or mapping of design elements to test cases. There are only class/test docstrings grouping tests by topic, but no explicit matrix artifact.

expectations.text: Every behavior rule (startup clamping, cruise rate-of-change, emergency shutdown, was_clamped flag) has at least one test
expectations.evidence: Startup clamping: TestStartupMode (lines 129-165) with min/max clamping tests. Cruise rate-of-change: TestRateOfChangeLimiting (lines 203-258) with multiple ROC tests. Emergency shutdown: TestEmergencyShutdownMode (lines 264-287). was_clamped flag: TestWasClampedFlag (lines 327-342).

expectations.text: Every error_handling entry (negative rate, negative elapsed_time, unrecognized mode) has a dedicated test
expectations.evidence: Negative rate: test_negative_requested_rate_treated_as_zero (line 296) and test_negative_requested_rate_in_startup_clamped_to_min (line 301). Negative elapsed_time: test_negative_elapsed_time_skips_roc (line 308). Unrecognized mode: test_unrecognised_mode_treated_as_emergency (line 315).

expectations.text: Boundary values tested for requested_rate (0.0, negative, at STARTUP_MIN, at STARTUP_MAX, at CRUISE_MAX)
expectations.evidence: 0.0: test_rate_zero_clamped_to_minimum (line 160), test_rate_at_zero_passes_through (line 180). Negative: test_negative_requested_rate_treated_as_zero (line 296). At STARTUP_MIN (10.0): test_rate_at_minimum_boundary (line 138). At STARTUP_MAX (50.0): test_rate_at_maximum_boundary (line 143). At CRUISE_MAX (200.0): test_rate_at_maximum_boundary in TestCruiseMode (line 185).

expectations.text: No test uses 'assert does not raise' or 'assert no exception' as its only assertion
expectations.evidence: Every test in the file asserts on concrete values (actual_rate, was_clamped, clamping_reason). No test uses 'assert does not raise', 'assert no exception', or pytest.raises as its only assertion.

expectations.text: Test names describe the scenario being tested, not just the method name
expectations.evidence: Test names are descriptive scenarios: test_rate_below_minimum_is_clamped_up, test_large_increase_is_limited, test_negative_requested_rate_treated_as_zero, test_emergency_resets_previous_rate_to_zero, test_roc_across_multiple_steps, etc. No test is named after a method like 'test_limit'.

expectations.text: Stateful behavior tested: rate-of-change limiting requires multiple sequential calls
expectations.evidence: test_roc_across_multiple_steps (line 231) makes two sequential calls verifying ramp-up behavior. test_decrease_rate_of_change_limited (line 221) first sets previous_rate to 100 then tests decrease. test_roc_with_zero_elapsed_time (line 249) establishes a rate then tests with 0ms elapsed. TestInternalState (line 368) verifies previous_rate tracking across calls.

expectations.text: Configuration variants tested: at least one test with non-default config values
expectations.evidence: TestCustomConfiguration class (lines 387-413) has four tests with non-default configs: custom startup_min_rate=20.0, startup_max_rate=30.0, cruise_max_rate=150.0, and max_rate_change=50.0.
